title: Add test for `/containers/(id or name)/wait` docker compat EP when for huge labels.
body:
This should be done once updated SELinux policies are in testing distributions.

/kind task

related to:
* https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16781

comments.body:
@matejvasek: The label(s) `kind/task` cannot be applied, because the repository doesn't have them.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16835):

>This should be done once updated SELinux policies are in testing distributions.
>
>/kind task
>
>related to:
>* https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16781


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

The test:
```diff
diff --git a/test/python/docker/compat/test_containers.py b/test/python/docker/compat/test_containers.py
index 96160941b..edeb67a34 100644
--- a/test/python/docker/compat/test_containers.py
+++ b/test/python/docker/compat/test_containers.py
@@ -3,6 +3,8 @@ Integration tests for exercising docker-py against Podman Service.
 """
 import io
 import tarfile
+import threading
+import time
 from typing import IO, List, Optional
 
 from docker import errors
@@ -260,3 +262,23 @@ class TestContainers(common.DockerTestCase):
                 ctr.remove()
             if vol is not None:
                 vol.remove(force=True)
+
+    def test_wait_next_exit(self):
+        ctr: Container = self.docker.containers.create(
+            image=constant.ALPINE,
+            name="test-exit",
+            command=["true"],
+            labels={"my-label": "0" * 127_000})
+
+        try:
+            def wait_and_start():
+                time.sleep(5)
+                ctr.start()
+
+            t = threading.Thread(target=wait_and_start)
+            t.start()
+            ctr.wait(condition="next-exit", timeout=300)
+            t.join()
+        finally:
+            ctr.stop()
+            ctr.remove(force=True)
```
Could you just open a PR for this rather then an issue?

@rhatdan in the end I added the test in original PR, just for now there is skip() and beginning of the test.
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16781/files#diff-6fd89cdd7fc873cd6e1ac88c4ef40ae5977f52766dcadcf6f8a9cc1793039fadR267
